data.title: Swiss Satellite Firm SWISSto12 SA Eyes IPO by 2027
data.description: Swiss satellite systems manufacturer SWISSto12 SA is contemplating an initial public offering (IPO) potentially as early as 2027, according to individuals with knowledge of the situation. Bloomberg posted on X, revealing that the company is exploring this strategic move to expand its operations and enhance its market presence. SWISSto12 SA, known for its innovative satellite technology, aims to leverage the IPO to secure additional funding and accelerate growth. The decision to go public is part of a broader plan to capitalize on increasing demand for satellite systems globally. The firm is currently assessing market conditions and preparing for the potential listing, which could significantly impact its future trajectory.

data.title: Hyperliquid Establishes DeFi Policy Center in Washington D.C.
data.description: Hyperliquid has launched a DeFi Policy Center in Washington D.C. to promote legal frameworks for decentralized finance adoption in the United States. According to NS3.AI, Jake Chervinsky will serve as CEO, concentrating on developing regulatory guidelines for perpetual derivatives. The center has received a donation of 1 million HYPE tokens, valued at approximately $28 million, and is in the process of recruiting for key leadership positions.

data.title: Moonwell has released an official statement regarding the oracle configuration error incident, and a governance proposal to fix it is planned to be released.
data.description: DeFi lending protocol Moonwell issued an official statement regarding the $1.78 million in bad debt caused by an oracle configuration error. The statement confirmed that a serious error in an oracle configuration caused the system to calculate the cbETH price incorrectly, using only the cbETH/ETH exchange rate instead of the expected price. This resulted in a cbETH price of approximately $1.12 instead of the true market price of approximately $2200. The erroneous price triggered a liquidation bot to liquidate cbETH collateralized positions. The attacker only needed to repay approximately $1 of debt to liquidate 1 cbETH, leading to the liquidation of a large number of borrowers' collateral and creating substantial bad debt. A small number of users also exploited the abnormal pricing to borrow large amounts of cbETH with extremely low collateral, artificially amplifying the protocol's bad debt. Ultimately, a total of 1096,317 cbETH was liquidated, most of which was bad debt denominated in cbETH. Because oracle repair requires a five-day governance vote and time-lock process, liquidation continues during this period. A related governance proposal for repair is currently planned for release.

data.title: U.S. Residential Construction Hits Five-Month High in December
data.description: New residential construction in the United States reached its highest level in five months during December. Bloomberg posted on X that homebuilders increased production, capitalizing on reduced borrowing costs. This surge in construction activity reflects a positive response from the housing sector to the more favorable financial conditions. The increase in building activity suggests optimism among developers, who are keen to leverage the current economic environment to meet housing demand.

data.title: CLOs and Financial Stability: An Analysis
data.description: The Long View, institutional investor, posted on X that Collateralized Loan Obligations (CLOs) have specific sector and ratings limits. The focus is primarily on Investment Grade (IG) securities, which are considered crucial for maintaining financial stability. According to the post, these factors suggest that CLOs do not pose a significant risk to the financial system.

data.title: Bitcoin treasury company Hyperscale Data launches strategic silver reserve plan, intending to purchase 100,000 ounces of silver.
data.description: Hyperscale Data, a Bitcoin treasury company listed on NYSE American, a subsidiary of the New York Stock Exchange, announced a strategic silver reserve program aimed at acquiring 100,000 ounces of silver over a period of time, with the goal of strengthening its balance sheet in the long term, similar to its Bitcoin holdings. According to the latest data from Hyperscale Data, the company's total Bitcoin holdings have increased to approximately 600 BTC, including approximately 554 BTC held by its wholly-owned subsidiary Sentinum, and approximately 46 BTC purchased from the open market by another subsidiary, ACG. (PRNewswire)

data.title: Hyperliquid launches DeFi lobbying group led by prominent crypto lawyers
data.description: The Hyperliquid Policy Center was established in Washington, D.C., to create a legal pathway for the widespread adoption of decentralized finance (DeFi) in the United States. Jake Chervinsky serves as the center's inaugural CEO, and founding team members include Brad Bourque and Salah Ghazzal. The center's primary mission is to develop a legal framework for perpetual derivatives. In addition, the Hyperliquid Foundation donated 1 million HYPE tokens, worth approximately $28 million, to the center. The center is currently recruiting a Chief of Staff, Director of Communications, and Director of Government Relations.

data.title: U.S. Durable Goods Orders Exceed Expectations in December
data.description: The U.S. durable goods orders excluding aircraft and non-defense capital goods rose by 0.6% in December, surpassing the anticipated 0.4%. According to Jin10, the previous value was revised from 0.40% to 0.8%. This data indicates a stronger-than-expected performance in the manufacturing sector, reflecting increased business investment and economic resilience.

data.title: Grayscale's Insights on Sui Network and GSUI
data.description:
Grayscale has shared insights on the Sui Network and its native token, GSUI. Grayscale posted on X, highlighting the importance of understanding the network's potential and the role of GSUI within it. The Sui Network is gaining attention for its innovative approach to blockchain technology, aiming to enhance scalability and efficiency.

The network's architecture is designed to support a wide range of decentralized applications, offering developers a robust platform for building and deploying projects. GSUI, as the native token, plays a crucial role in the network's ecosystem, facilitating transactions and incentivizing participation.

Grayscale's analysis emphasizes the significance of the Sui Network in the evolving blockchain landscape, noting its potential impact on the industry. The insights provided aim to inform stakeholders and investors about the opportunities and challenges associated with the network and its token.

As the blockchain sector continues to grow, networks like Sui are expected to contribute to the advancement of decentralized technologies, offering new possibilities for innovation and development.

data.title: U.S. President Donald Trump's Efforts to Boost Coal Industry
data.description: Lanny Erdos, whose father worked as a coal miner in Ohio, is now at the forefront of U.S. President Donald Trump's initiative to rejuvenate coal demand and production. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Erdos's role in the administration's push to support the coal industry. The effort comes amid broader discussions on energy policy and environmental concerns, as the administration seeks to balance economic growth with sustainable practices. Erdos's involvement underscores the personal connection and commitment to the coal sector, reflecting the administration's focus on revitalizing traditional energy sources.

data.title: More than 50% of U.S. investors are worried about facing IRS penalties due to new regulations implemented this year.
data.description: A survey of 1,000 U.S. cryptocurrency holders revealed that over 50% of investors are concerned about potential IRS penalties due to new transparency rules implemented this year. Under the new rules, cryptocurrency exchanges like Coinbase must file Form 1099-DA with the IRS, reporting all sales and exchanges of digital assets during 2025. Andrew Duca, founder of Awaken Tax, stated that exchanges can only provide sales revenue information and cannot report the acquisition cost and tax basis of assets. Cryptocurrency holders will need to supplement the missing acquisition cost and actual tax basis themselves using the updated Form 8949. Currently, the cryptocurrency tax compliance rate is below 20%, and the new rules aim to increase that rate.

data.title: Apollo Global Management Fund Extends $250 Million Loan to Irish Fintech Unicorn
data.description: An Apollo Global Management-backed fund has provided a $250 million loan to an Irish fintech unicorn. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that this financial support will enable the fintech company to extend loans to small businesses seeking capital. The move is expected to bolster the fintech's lending capabilities, allowing it to meet the growing demand for business financing. This development underscores the ongoing interest and investment in the fintech sector, particularly in supporting small and medium-sized enterprises.

data.title: Liberty Global to Acquire Vodafone's Stake in VodafoneZiggo for €1 Billion
data.description: Liberty Global has reached an agreement to purchase Vodafone Group's share in the Dutch telecommunications firm VodafoneZiggo for €1 billion in cash. Bloomberg posted on X that the deal also includes a stake in a newly formed holding company. This acquisition will allow Liberty Global to consolidate its position in the Dutch market by gaining full control over VodafoneZiggo. The transaction is expected to enhance operational efficiencies and drive growth in the region. The move is part of Liberty Global's broader strategy to expand its footprint in Europe and strengthen its telecommunications portfolio.

data.title: U.S. Tax Refunds Could Inject $150B of Liquidity Into Crypto Markets
data.description: An influx of U.S. tax refunds could provide a near-term liquidity boost for cryptocurrency markets, potentially lifting retail participation as prices hover near key technical levels.U.S. taxpayers are expected to receive around $150 billion in tax refunds by the end of March 2026, supported by recent tax cuts and fiscal incentives, according to analysis cited by NS3.AI.Historically, a portion of tax refunds has flowed into risk assets, including equities and cryptocurrencies, as households deploy excess cash beyond immediate consumption. Analysts say the timing is notable, as the refund window coincides with crypto markets trading near important breakout and resistance zones.Market observers argue that an incremental rise in retail inflows—layered on top of existing institutional activity—could amplify volatility and increase the likelihood of short squeezes, particularly in assets with crowded derivatives positioning.While not all refunds are expected to reach crypto markets, even a modest allocation could meaningfully impact short-term demand and trading volumes, given the sector’s relatively smaller size compared with traditional asset classes. Analysts caution that the effect will depend on broader risk sentiment and macro conditions but view the refund season as a potential catalyst worth monitoring.

data.title: Sports prediction market Novig raises $75 million in Series B funding, led by Pantera Capital.
data.description: Sports prediction marketplace Novig announced the completion of a $75 million Series B funding round, led by Pantera Capital. This round brings Novig's valuation to a guidance value of $500 million. Novig states that it is a prediction market platform focused on the sports sector. Founded by Jacob Fortinsky and Kelechi Ukah, the company aims to challenge the traditional sports betting market with a peer-to-peer (P2P) trading model. The platform currently operates on a lottery model and is applying for regulatory approval from the U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission. Novig charges no commission to retail investors, generating revenue through fees charged to institutional participants. The funds raised will be used to expand its business in the sports prediction market. (Fortune Magazine)

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | China's Export Growth Driven by Factors Beyond U.S. Trade Levies
data.description: A study by the European Central Bank reveals that U.S. President Donald Trump's trade tariffs are not the primary factor behind China's increased exports to other regions. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that the research indicates other elements are contributing to China's export expansion. The study suggests that China's trade strategies and global economic conditions play significant roles in this growth. Despite the tariffs, China's export activities have continued to thrive, driven by various underlying factors. The findings challenge the notion that U.S. trade policies are the sole drivers of China's export dynamics, pointing to a more complex interplay of global trade influences.
